edges.description: Defective Gs alpha signaling blunts cAMP response to PTH in proximal renal tubules.
Resistance may extend to other Gs alpha-coupled endocrine pathways including TSH and GHRH.
Reduced GNAS signaling in bone and adipose lineages drives Albright hereditary osteodystrophy features.
intermediate_mechanisms: altered Gs alpha-cAMP signaling in mesenchymal lineages

edges.description: Reduced renal phosphate excretion causes chronic phosphate retention.
Impaired renal response to PTH contributes to persistent low serum calcium.
Secondary hyperparathyroidism develops as parathyroid output rises to compensate.
Severe calcium-phosphate imbalance destabilizes neuromuscular function.
edges.target: Mineral Ion Imbalance and Neuromuscular Excitability

edges.description: Increased neuromuscular excitability causes cramps, spasms, and carpopedal signs.
edges.source: Mineral Ion Imbalance and Neuromuscular Excitability

edges.description: Marked hypocalcemia can precipitate focal or generalized seizures.
edges.source: Mineral Ion Imbalance and Neuromuscular Excitability

edges.description: TSH resistance can lead to biochemical and clinical hypothyroidism.
Combined endocrine resistance and skeletal effects can limit linear growth.
Disordered endochondral growth shortens metacarpals and other tubular bones.
Ectopic dermal and subcutaneous ossification forms firm palpable nodules.
Altered GNAS-dependent metabolic regulation contributes to early-onset adiposity.
intermediate_mechanisms: MC4R-related appetite and energy homeostasis signaling

edges.description: Some patients show neurodevelopmental effects with learning or global delay.

edges.source: Calcium and Active Vitamin D Replacement

nodes.description: Albright hereditary osteodystrophy reflects altered developmental signaling in bone, adipose tissue, and other mesenchymal lineages, partially independent of biochemical severity.

nodes.description: Calcium supplementation combined with active vitamin D (calcitriol or alfacalcidol) to correct hypocalcemia and hyperphosphatemia. Standard of care for PHP1A and PHP1B; correction of hypocalcemia resolves tetany, seizures, and dyskinesias.

evidence.reference_title: Variable Bone Phenotypes in Patients with Pseudohypoparathyroidism.
evidence.snippet: Pseudohypoparathyroidism (PHP) is a disorder caused by mutations and/or epigenetic changes at the complex GNAS locus.

nodes.description: Pathogenic variants or epigenetic abnormalities at the GNAS locus reduce functional Gs alpha signaling. Because GNAS expression is parent-of-origin dependent in selected tissues, maternal defects disproportionately impair receptor signaling in renal proximal tubules and endocrine targets.

biological_processes: adenylate cyclase-modulating G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway
